description: §12.4 — a server-classified function (escalated by a ?{} SQL context) calls a client-only function (`flashTitle`, which touches the browser-only `document` global). Route analysis places `flashTitle` exclusively on the client, so the server fn cannot call it: E-ROUTE-002 fires as a hard error. Guards the soundness hole where the client-only callee was silently escalated to the server (Trigger 5) and its `document.*` code shipped into the SERVER bundle.
rationale: §12.4: a server-escalated function's static call graph SHALL NOT contain a direct or transitive call to a client-only function; the compiler SHALL emit E-ROUTE-002. A server function runs in the Bun process where `document`/`window` do not exist, so the call cannot execute server-side.
